title: Method and device for detecting a roadside object
abstract: The invention relates to a method for detecting a roadside object (20), in particular in form of a three dimensional object like a curb, in the vicinity of a vehicle (10), the method comprising the steps of â¢ recording at least one data set comprising a plurality of data points associated with a region in a lateral vicinity of the vehicle (10), the region at least partially including at least one wheel (16, 18) of the vehicle (10) and the road surface, wherein each data point includes information regarding its relative position with respect to the vehicle (10); â¢ classifying each data point according to whether it corresponds to the at least one wheel (16, 18), to the road surface or to the roadside object (20); â¢ determining a distance between a data point classified as corresponding to the at least one wheel (16, 18) and a data point classified as corresponding to the roadside obstacle (20); and â¢ generating a signal if the distance is below at least one threshold value. The invention further relates to a sensor device (12) adapted for performing such a method and a vehicle (10) with such a sensor device (12).
claims: Claims Claims 1. Method for detecting a roadside object (20), in particular in form of a three dimensional object like a curb, in the vicinity of a vehicle (10) on a road surface, the method comprising the steps of â¢ recording at least one data set comprising a plurality of data points associated with a region in a lateral vicinity of the vehicle (10), the region at least partially including at least one wheel (16, 18) of the vehicle (10) and the road surface, wherein each data point includes information regarding its relative position with respect to the vehicle (10); â¢ classifying each data point according to whether it corresponds to the at least one wheel (16, 18), to the road surface or to the roadside object (20); â¢ determining a distance between a data point classified as corresponding to the at least one wheel (16, 18) and a data point classified as corresponding to the roadside obstacle (20); and â¢ generating a signal if the distance is below at least one threshold value. 2. Method according to claim 1, wherein the recording step is performed by a device (12) mounted to the vehicle (10) and comprising at least one sensor, which is preferably selected from a group comprising at least one camera, time-of-flight camera, a Laser sensor and/or LIDAR sensor. 3. Method according to claim 2, wherein the recording step comprises moving of the sensor relative to the vehicle (10), in particular for scanning the region at least in one direction. 4. Method according to claim 3, wherein a scanning interval is set, preferably depending on the motion of the vehicle (10), with the scanning interval in particular being about 1/10 second when the vehicle (10) is in motion. 5. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the region is suited for recording data points from the front to the rear wheels in parallel to the longitudinal axis on the vehicle (10), which defines the x direction, preferably on the driver side and/or the non driver side of the vehicle (10). 6. Method according to claim 5, wherein the recoding angel in the x direction is up to 180Â°, in particular about 120Â°, and/or the recoding range in the x direction is up to 10 m, preferably around 5 m. 7. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the region is suited for recording data points perpendicular to the longitudinal axis on the vehicle (10), which defines the y direction, preferably within a range of up to 30 cm and/or a recoding angel of up to 45Â°, in particular of about 15Â°. 8. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the region is suited for recording data points for determining a height above the road surface, which defines the z direction, preferably within a range of up to 30 cm, in particular of up to 10 cm. 9. Method according to claim 5 or 6 and claim 7 or 8, wherein the sensor has a viewing range in x direction and is moved for scanning the region in y and/or z direction. 10. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sensor is calibrated to cover the region, which preferably can be selected and/or changed. 11. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the classifying step comprises the allocation of at least a part of the plurality of data points to a data grid (20), with the data grid (20) being at least 2 dimensional. 12. Method according to claim 11, wherein data grid (20) comprises n rows and m columns, wherein preferably n is selected to be between 1 and 20 in the x direction, and/or preferably m is selected to be between 10 and 100 in the y direction. 13. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each data point includes information characteristic for â¢ a relative elevation or height above the road surface and/or â¢ a color and/or â¢ a change as a function of time, with preferably each data point being classified based on said information. 14. Method according to any one of the claims 11 to 13, wherein each grid point of the grid (22) is classified either into â¢ a first category Ml, comprising data points which correspond to one of the wheels (16, 18) of the vehicle (10), â¢ a second category M2 corresponding to the road surface, and â¢ a third category M3 corresponding to the object (20). 15. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the generating step comprises â¢ generating an audible, visible and/or haptic warning signal for the driver of the vehicle (10) and/or â¢ generating a signal to a driver assist system, in particular a signal instructing the driver assist system to perform a steering and/or braking action. 16. Method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein generating the signal in form of a warning signal â¢ is suppressed if a velocity of the vehicle (10) is lower than a predetermined value and/or â¢ depends on the motion of the vehicle (10) and/or said information and/or the determined distance. 17. Device (12) for detecting a roadside object (20), in particular a curb, in the vicinity of a vehicle (10) on a road surface, with a control circuit adapted to perform the method according to any one of the preceding claims. 18. Device (12) according to claim 17, being integrated at least partly in an external rear view device (14) and/or a door sill. 19. Vehicle (10) with a device (12) according to claim 17 or 17. 20. Vehicle (10) according to claim 18, wherein the control unit is comprised by the central vehicle control system.
